items.title: When Words Are Safe But Actions Kill: Probing Physical Danger Beyond Text Safety in Hidden-State Risk Space
items.abstract: Large language models (LLMs) increasingly serve as high-level planners for embodied agents, where linguistically benign instructions can become unsafe once grounded in the physical world. We study whether this physically grounded danger is the same safety problem as ordinary text-level content danger. Through hidden-state direction analysis and random-split null tests, we show that content danger (CD) and physical danger (PD) form separable signals in LLM representations across Qwen2.5-3B/7B/14B/32B, Phi-3.5 and SmolLM2. Building on the CD/PD separability, we propose PRISM, a single-layer L2-regularized logistic probe over full hidden states. PRISM achieves 86.2--87.7\% accuracy on SafeAgentBench with 11.7--13.7\% FPR, while same-scale LLM judges over-block safe tasks at 24.7--39.0\% FPR. We further introduce PhysicalSafetyBench-1K (PSB-1K), a contrastive benchmark of 1{,}000 physical-risk pairs without direct harm keywords, to test whether methods detect physically grounded danger rather than explicit unsafe wording. On PSB-1K, PRISM reaches 99.6\% accuracy and 0.7\% FPR, whereas a Qwen2.5-3B judge rejects 67.8\% of safe tasks. PRISM also replicates on SafeText and EARBench, supporting hidden-state probing as a representation-level method for physical safety beyond text moderation.
items.llm_evidence_en: probes physical danger in hidden-state risk space for embodied agent safety
items.llm_evidence_cn: 在隐状态风险空间中探测具身代理的物理危险
items.llm_evidence: 在隐状态风险空间中探测具身代理的物理危险
items.canonical_evidence: 在隐状态风险空间中探测具身代理的物理危险
items.llm_tldr_en: Shows physical danger and content danger are separable in LLM hidden states; proposes PRISM probe achieving 86-87% accuracy on SafeAgentBench.
items.llm_tldr_cn: LLM作为具身代理的高层规划器时，语言上安全的指令在物理世界中可能危险。本文通过隐状态方向分析发现内容危险与物理危险在表征上可分离，基于此提出PRISM线性探针，在SafeAgentBench上达到86-87%准确率且仅需少量训练。该工作为物理层面的安全评估提供了新视角。
items.llm_tldr: LLM作为具身代理的高层规划器时，语言上安全的指令在物理世界中可能危险。本文通过隐状态方向分析发现内容危险与物理危险在表征上可分离，基于此提出PRISM线性探针，在SafeAgentBench上达到86-87%准确率且仅需少量训练。该工作为物理层面的安全评估提供了新视角。

items.title: SMC-ES: Automated synthesis of formally verified control policies
items.abstract: The deployment of autonomous cyber-physical systems in safety-critical environments requires closed-loop control strategies (i.e., policies) that are not only performant but also provably safe and robust. While learning-based methodologies such as Reinforcement Learning offer flexible and scalable approaches to automatically synthesize such controllers, they typically lack the formal guarantees necessary for safe deployment. To bridge this gap, we propose a novel simulation-based methodology to automatically synthesize policies with formal guarantees regarding performance, safety, and robustness specifications. Specifically, given a set of properties to verify, a confidence parameter $δ$ and an allowable failure probability $\varepsilon$, our method guarantees that the synthesized policy comes with a certificate: with confidence at least $1 - δ$, the probability of encountering a scenario where the given properties are violated is at most $\varepsilon$. We demonstrate the feasibility of our approach by developing SMC-ES, an algorithm that integrates Evolutionary Strategies with Statistical Model Checking-based verification. We evaluate SMC-ES on a suite of continuous control tasks using Gymnasium and Safety Gymnasium testbeds. Results show that, at the price of a sustainable increase in computational cost, our algorithm provides formal guarantees regarding performance, safety, and robustness specifications, while performing competitively against leading model-free Deep Reinforcement Learning (DRL) and Safe-DRL baselines.
items.llm_evidence_en: synthesizes policies with formal safety guarantees for CPS
items.llm_evidence_cn: 为安全关键系统合成具有形式化保证的控制策略
items.llm_evidence: 为安全关键系统合成具有形式化保证的控制策略
items.canonical_evidence: 为安全关键系统合成具有形式化保证的控制策略
items.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a simulation-based method to automatically synthesize control policies with formal guarantees on performance, safety, and robustness using reinforcement learning, ensuring safe deployment in safety-critical environments.
items.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文提出了一种基于仿真的方法，利用强化学习自动合成具有形式化保证的控制策略，包括性能、安全性和鲁棒性规范。针对安全关键环境，该方法通过概率验证确保策略的可靠部署，弥合了学习型控制器缺乏形式化保证的差距。
items.llm_tldr: 该论文提出了一种基于仿真的方法，利用强化学习自动合成具有形式化保证的控制策略，包括性能、安全性和鲁棒性规范。针对安全关键环境，该方法通过概率验证确保策略的可靠部署，弥合了学习型控制器缺乏形式化保证的差距。
